revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Hibita-jinja]][[ja:比々多神社]] {{Expand Japanese|比々多神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hibita Shrine | native_name = 比々多神社 | image = Hibita jinja Haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hibita Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.40130555555555|139.28394444444444}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|es|Toyo-kumo-no-no-kami|ja|トヨクモノ|ru|Тоё-кумоно-но ками|uk|Тойо кумоно но камі|zh|豐斟渟尊|qid=Q4387158|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto}} | website = {{Official website|http://hibita.jp/}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hibita Shrine'''|比々多神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|San-no-Miya|lt=San-no-Miya|qid=Q134917303|1=San-no-Miya}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q734520|ltq=Q734520|qid=Q734520|1=Isehara, Kanagawa}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.40130555555555|139.28394444444444}} == official website (P856) == * http://hibita.jp/ ** Language of work: {{ill|Q5287|ltq=Q5287|qid=Q5287|1=Japanese language}} == image (P18) == * Hibita jinja Haiden.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|es|Toyo-kumo-no-no-kami|ja|トヨクモノ|ru|Тоё-кумоно-но ками|uk|Тойо кумоно но камі|zh|豐斟渟尊|qid=Q4387158|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto}}https://hibita.jp/hibita/ ** P1932: 豊斟野尊 ** P1932: 豊斟渟尊 * {{ill|Q11572729|ltq=Q11572729|qid=Q11572729|1=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto}}https://hibita.jp/hibita/ ** P1932: 天櫛明玉命 ** P1932: 天明玉命 * {{ill|Q11596349|ltq=Q11596349|qid=Q11596349|1=Wakahiru-me}}https://hibita.jp/hibita/ ** P1932: 稚日女命 ** P1932: 稚日女尊 * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|lt=Yamato Takeru|arz|ياماتو تاكيرو|be|Ямата Такэру|de|Yamatotakeru|en|Yamato Takeru|es|Yamato Takeru|fa|یاماتو تاکه‌رو|fr|Yamato Takeru|hu|Jamatotakeru|id|Yamato Takeru|it|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|ko|야마토타케루|pt|Yamato Takeru|ru|Ямато Такэру|sh|Yamato Takeru|simple|Yamato Takeru|sq|Jamato Takeru|sr|Јамато Такеру|sv|Yamato Takeru|tr|Yamato Takeru|uk|Ямато Такеру|ur|یاماتو تاکیرو|vi|Yamato Takeru|zh_min_nan|Yamato Takeru|zh|日本武尊|qid=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}}https://hibita.jp/hibita/ ** P1932: 日本武命 ** P1932: 日本武尊 == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}https://hibita.jp/hibita/ ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == legal form (P1454) == * {{ill|religious corporation|lt=religious corporation|ja|宗教法人|qid=Q7311343|1=religious corporation}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Q210684|ltq=Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} == official blog URL (P1581) == * http://www.hibita.jp/weblog/ ** Language of work: {{ill|Q5287|ltq=Q5287|qid=Q5287|1=Japanese language}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Japan|lt=Japan|ab|Иапониа|ace|Jeupang|ady|Японие|af|Japan|als|Japan|alt|Јопон|ami|Ripon|am|ጃፓን|ang|Iapan|anp|जापान|an|Chapón|arc|ܝܦܢ|ar|اليابان|arnews|تصنيف:اليابان|arquote|اليابان|ary|الجاپون|arz|اليابان|ast|Xapón|as|জাপান|atj|Japan|avk|Nipona|av|Япония|awa|जापान|ay|Nihun|azb|ژاپون|az|Yaponiya|azquote|Yaponiya|ban|Jepang|bar|Japan|bat_smg|Japuonėjė|ba|Япония|bbc|Japang|bcl|Hapon|bdr|Gipun|be_x_old|Японія|be|Японія|bew|Jepang|bg|Япония|bgnews|Категория:Япония|bh|जापान|bi|Japan|bjn|Japang|blk|ဂျပန်ခမ်းထီ|bm|Japan|bn|জাপান|bnquote|জাপান|bnvoyage|জাপান|bo|ཉི་ཧོང་།|bpy|জাপান|br|Japan|bs|Japan|bsnews|Kategorija:Japan|btm|Jepang|bug|ᨍᨛᨄ|bxr|Япон ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * ひびたじんじゃ == X (Twitter) username (P2002) == * hibita3 ** P6552: 274057069 ** Start time: {'time': '+2011-03-00T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 10, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} == Instagram username (P2003) == * hibitajinja == Facebook username (P2013) == * hibita38 == area (P2046) == * {'amount': '+3000', 'unit': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q706688'} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4021005003965 == Commons category (P373) == * Hibita-jinja == panoramic view (P4291) == * Hibita shrine - Kanagawa - 2023 September 12 various 13 09 29 650000.jpeg == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039721|ltq=Q135039721|qid=Q135039721|1=Hihitano Shrine}} ** P3831: {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} ** P2868: {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '神奈川県伊勢原市三ノ宮1472', 'language': 'ja'}https://hibita.jp/access/ ** P7452: {{ill|Q29509043|ltq=Q29509043|qid=Q29509043|1=official}} * {'text': '神奈川県伊勢原市三ノ宮1468', 'language': 'ja'} == day in year for periodic occurrence (P837) == * {{ill|Q2526|ltq=Q2526|qid=Q2526|1=April 22}} ** P3831: {{ill|Q11385469|ltq=Q11385469|qid=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} == social media followers (P8687) == * {'amount': '+1705', 'unit': '1'} ** P6552: 274057069 ** Point in time: {'time': '+2021-01-08T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * HIbita jinja Gate.jpg {{commons category|Category:Hibita-jinja}} [[JA:比々多神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11547364}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Hibita Shrine |画像=[[ファイル:Hibita jinja Haiden.jpg|270px|Worship hall]]
Worship hall |所在地={{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|qid=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Isehara|Q734520|qid=Q734520|1=Isehara, Kanagawa}} Sannomiya 1472 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Toyokumono|Q4387158|qid=Q4387158|lt=Toyokuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame no Akaru Tama no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|qid=Q11596349|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|1=Wakahiru-me}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Sagami Province#Shrines|Q1053732|qid=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Sannomiya|1=Sagami Province}}
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=(trad.) 6th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|qid=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} (655 BCE) |本殿={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|lt=three-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名=Kanmuri Daimyōjin, Sannomiya-sama |札所等= |例祭={{ill|lt=April 21|Q2525|qid=Q2525|1=April 21}} and {{ill|lt=April 22|Q2526|qid=Q2526|lt=22|1=April 22}} |神事={{ill|lt=Kōno-machi (Kokufu Festival)|Q11420919|qid=Q11420919|1=Kokufu-sai}} ({{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|qid=Q2550|1=May 5}})
Sake Festival (late November)
See also {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=#Festivals|1=UNKNOWN}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|label=Hibita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:HIbita jinja Gate.jpg|250px|right|thumb|Torii]] [[ファイル:Hibita jinja Honden.jpg|250px|right|thumb|Main sanctuary]] [[ファイル:Sannomiya Kyoudo-hakubutsukan.jpg|250px|right|thumb|Sannomiya Local Museum]] '''Hibita Shrine''' (ひびたじんじゃ) is an old shrine of the former {{ill|lt=Sagami Province|Q1053732|qid=Q1053732|1=Sagami Province}} in Sannomiya, {{ill|lt=Isehara|Q734520|qid=Q734520|1=Isehara, Kanagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|qid=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and today it is a {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture shrine list#Shrines designated by the Kanagawa Association of Shrines|Q11588941|qid=Q11588941|lt=heihaku-messenger visiting shrine|1=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office Designated Shrine}} under the Kanagawa {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q11590703|qid=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}. In older times it was also called '''Kanmuri Daimyōjin'''. It is considered to be '''Hibita Shrine''' listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' of {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}) (one of the 13 Engishiki shrines of Sagami Province, minor shrine), but as noted {{ill|lt=#Ancient period|UNKNOWN|lt=below}}, there are also {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō#disputed shrines|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=competing claims|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. It is one of the five Sagami shrines participating in the old-Sagami traditional {{ill|lt=festival|Q132241|qid=Q132241|lt=rite|1=Festival}}, the {{ill|lt=Kokufu Festival|Q11420919|qid=Q11420919|lt=Kōno-machi|1=Kokufu-sai}}, held every {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|qid=Q2550|1=May 5}} at Kamisoroi-yama in Kokufu-Hongō, {{ill|lt=Ōiso|Q385023|qid=Q385023|1=Ōiso}}, and corresponds to the province’s {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya#Ninomiya and Sannomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}. The place name “Sannomiya” derives from this shrine, and it has long also been called “'''Sannomiya-sama'''.” === Enshrined deities === Principal deities * Toyokumunu-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Toyokumono|Q4387158|qid=Q4387158|lt=Toyokuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame no Akaru Tama no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|qid=Q11596349|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|1=Wakahiru-me}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258}} {{ill|lt=Aiden deities|Q1107120|qid=Q1107120|1=Andrea Di Stefano}} (accompanying hall deities) * Ōsakatoke-no-Kami ({{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}}) * Kosakatoke-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}) The above are regarded as the current deities, but according to 'Nihon no Kamigami: Shrines and Sacred Sites 11 Kantō'Edited by Ken'ichi Tanigawa, 'Nihon no Kamigami: Shrines and Sacred Sites 11 Kantō' (Hakusuisha, Dec. 1984)., there were once four theories. Three are generally similar to the current deities, but notable is the inscription on the temple bell at the shrine (now lost; for the reason see “{{ill|lt=#History|UNKNOWN|lt=History}}” below) described in '{{ill|lt=Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudokikō|Q11503942|qid=Q11503942|1=Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudoki-kō}} Vol. 50'Included in Itojin Ashida (ed.), 'Dai Nihon Chishi Taikei 17: Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudokikō Vol. 3' (Yūzankaku, Sept. 1962)., saying the spirit of Someya Tarō-dayū Tokitada, Kamakura military commander in the {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|qid=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō era|1=Tenpyō}}, was enshrinedAccording to 'Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudokikō Vol. 50,' this is said to be the father of {{ill|lt=Ryōben|Q720819|qid=Q720819|1=Rōben}}, founder of Mount Ōyama in the 'Ōyama Engi.'. However, that same book states that this is hard to trust because it differs greatly from current shrine tradition. === History === === Origin === According to 'Hibita Denki' written in {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|qid=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1834|Q7591|qid=Q7591|1=1834}})Included in Shintō Taikei Editorial Committee (ed.), 'Shintō Taikei: Shrine Section 16 Suruga, Izu, Kai, Sagami' (Shintō Taikei Editorial Committee, Mar. 1980)., the shrine was established during {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|qid=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}’s pacification of the realm to protect the people. According to 'Hibita Shrine Worship Guide''Hibita Shrine Worship Guide' is a pamphlet distributed at the shrine., this was in Jimmu 6 ({{ill|lt=655 BCE|Q720071|qid=Q720071|1=655 BC}}): people selected this place, where rituals had long been performed, as best, and enshrined Toyokuninushi-no-Mikoto as the spirit of Japan with {{ill|lt=Mount Ōyama (Kanagawa)|Q386518|qid=Q386518|lt=Mount Ōyama|1=Mount Ōyama (Kanagawa)}} as the {{ill|lt=shintai mountain|Q1595289|qid=Q1595289|1=Sacred mountains}}. Meanwhile, from ruins and artifacts excavated in and near the precincts, a {{ill|lt=standing stone|Q11598071|qid=Q11598071|lt=standing stone|1=standing stone}} within a mid-{{ill|lt=Jōmon period|Q52824|qid=Q52824|1=Jōmon period}} {{ill|lt=stone circle|Q1935728|qid=Q1935728|1=Stone circle}} is estimated to be a ritual site. The shrine side estimates that the origin of this shrine may go back to primal Jōmon {{ill|lt=mountain worship|Q93697439|qid=Q93697439|1=Mountain worship}} over 10,000 years ago, potentially making it one of eastern Japan’s oldest shrines. This shrine stands on the southeastern foot of Mount Ōyama; 'Nihon no Kamigami: 11 Kantō' also considers that it was likely originally a shrine for distant worship of Mount Ōyama. === Ancient period === According to 'Hibita Denki', in {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|qid=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 7 ({{ill|lt=91 BCE|Q40968|qid=Q40968|1=91 BC}}), shrine lands and {{ill|lt=kannbe|Q11589711|qid=Q11589711|lt=kannbe households|1=Kanbe (social class)}} were granted; in the 8th month of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|qid=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} 27 ({{ill|lt=3 BCE|Q39821|qid=Q39821|1=3 BC}}), by imperial order, the Jingikan offered bows and arrows. According to 'Hibita Shrine Worship Guide', in {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|qid=Q1077930|1=Taika (era)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=645|Q31040|qid=Q31040|1=645}}), two deities, Ōsakatoke and Kosakatoke, were enshrined together, and at that time a {{ill|lt=Sue ware|Q7634351|qid=Q7634351|1=Sue pottery}} vessel called “Uzuramika”Read "uzuramika." It leaves the main sanctuary only for the Sake Festival ritual in late November each year. It is designated an Important Cultural Property of Kanagawa Prefecture. was deposited. Also according to the guide, in {{ill|lt=Empress Jitō|Q232026|qid=Q232026|1=Empress Jitō}} 6 ({{ill|lt=692|Q23477|qid=Q23477|1=692}}), provincial governor Fuse no Ason Shikofuchi repaired the shrine buildings and dedicated a pair of {{ill|lt=komainu|Q20155846|qid=Q20155846|1=Komainu}}This wooden pair of komainu is designated an Important Cultural Property of Kanagawa Prefecture.. In {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|qid=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 15 ({{ill|lt=743|Q23604|qid=Q23604|1=743}}), Ki no Ason Masumaro, a descendant of {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|qid=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}, became first chief priest, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|qid=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}} granted {{ill|lt=shōen|Q1887569|qid=Q1887569|lt=estate land|1=Shōen}}. According to the guide, in {{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|qid=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}} 9 ({{ill|lt=832|Q23900|qid=Q23900|1=832}}), as imperial envoy, provincial governor Tachibana no Ason Minetsugu bestowed by order of {{ill|lt=Emperor Junna|Q374136|qid=Q374136|1=Emperor Junna}} the divine title “'''Kanmuri Daimyōjin'''” of the {{ill|lt=Sagami Province|Q1053732|qid=Q1053732|1=Sagami Province}} {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|qid=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}}A similar tradition appears in the 'National Shrine Directory Vol. 1' (July 1977), though some dates differ.. The '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}) lists “Hibita Shrine” as one of four minor shrines in {{ill|lt=Ōsumi District (Sagami)|Q11432367|qid=Q11432367|1=Ōsumi district}}, Sagami Province. But according to 'Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudokikō Vol. 50', besides this shrine, {{ill|lt=Koyasu Myōjin Shrine|Q11547458|qid=Q11547458|lt=Koyasu Myōjin Shrine|1=Hibita Shrine (Q11547458)}} in Kamikasuya village (now Kamikasuya, Isehara) is also said to have been the Shikinaisha “Hibita Shrine.” The same book introduces a story told by this shrine’s chief priest: a plaque inscribed “Hibita Shrine” was lent to the Koyasu shrine priest and never returned, and Koyasu shrine then displayed it and claimed Shikinaisha status. It then says there is no evidence for this story, and since there is no proof either this shrine or Koyasu shrine is Engishiki “Hibita Shrine,” it is difficult to judge which one is correct. In 'Nihon no Kamigami: 11 Kantō', considering that this site overlooks an ancient official road and is a strong candidate location for the second-phase {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|qid=Q1629088|1=Kokufu}} of Sagami, that shrine tradition says it was the sōsha when the provincial capital was there, and judging from red-seal documents issued to both shrines in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, this shrine is considered likely the Engishiki “Hibita Shrine.” === Medieval period === In {{ill|lt=Genryaku|Q1194250|qid=Q1194250|1=Genryaku}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|qid=Q19728|1=1184}}), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|qid=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} carried out major reconstruction, and in {{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|qid=Q1194415|1=Bunji (era)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1185|Q19729|qid=Q19729|1=1185}}), submitted a prayer document for peace under heaven. Also, the entry for August 9, {{ill|lt=Kenkyū|Q1193933|qid=Q1193933|1=Kenkyū}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1192|Q19739|qid=Q19739|1=1192}}), in the '{{ill|lt=Azuma Kagami|Q1042324|qid=Q1042324|1=Azuma Kagami}}' states Yoritomo prayed at “Sannomiya Kanmuri Daimyōjin” for safe childbirth of {{ill|lt=Hōjō Masako|Q463961|qid=Q463961|1=Hōjō Masako}}, and dedicated a {{ill|lt=sacred horse|Q11591047|qid=Q11591047|1=Shinme}}. === Early modern period === According to 'Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudokikō Vol. 50', around the {{ill|lt=Meiō|Q1193841|qid=Q1193841|lt=Meiō era|1=Meiō}} ({{ill|lt=1492|Q6679|qid=Q6679|1=1492}}–{{ill|lt=1501|Q6131|qid=Q6131|1=1501}}), war fire caused loss of shrine lands; shrine officials and affiliated monks dispersed and the shrine greatly declined. The name of Kando villagePresent-day Kando, Isehara. is said to preserve the old name from when it was a sealed household area. The same source says that in early {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|qid=Q6678|1=1573}}–{{ill|lt=1593|Q6772|qid=Q6772|1=1593}}), the shrine site was moved from Racchi-menNear present Eikō Jogakuen Junior College of Horticulture in Sannomiya, Isehara. to its current location, and the deity transferred to a small shrine there. In the 11th month of Tenshō 19 ({{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|qid=Q6766|1=1591}}), a vermilion-seal document from {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|qid=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted 10 {{ill|lt=koku|Q855048|qid=Q855048|1=Koku}} of shrine land. 'Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudokikō Vol. 50' says this finally enabled the shrine’s recovery. Thereafter it received donations from successive shoguns into the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period. === Modern and contemporary period === In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was designated {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system#Miscellaneous shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} and became general tutelary shrine of 16 villages. In Meiji 41 ({{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|qid=Q2056|1=1908}}), it was designated a shrine for official offerings of sacred food and cloth. It is now a {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture shrine list#Shrines designated by the Kanagawa Association of Shrines|Q11588941|qid=Q11588941|lt=heihaku-messenger visiting shrine|1=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office Designated Shrine}} under Kanagawa Shrine Office. The inscribed temple bell said to have enshrined Someya Tarō-dayū Tokitada was lost during {{ill|lt=Pacific War|Q184425|qid=Q184425|1=Pacific War}} wartime metal requisition. The current bell was made in {{ill|lt=1950|Q18597|qid=Q18597|1=1950}} (Shōwa 25). In {{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|qid=Q18585|1=1953}} (Shōwa 28), the Sannomiya Local Museum was attached by former {{ill|lt=chief priest|Q11453500|qid=Q11453500|1=Gūji}} Nagai Sanji. === Festivals === * January 1: {{ill|lt=Saitan-sai|Q11546824|qid=Q11546824|1=Saitan-sai}}, New Year grand prayers * January 2: Shōkon-sha memorial festival * January 14: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Dondoyaki|1=UNKNOWN}} * February 3: {{ill|lt=Setsubun|Q746798|qid=Q746798|1=Setsubun}} Tsuina-sai * February 17: {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|qid=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} * March 17: Doll thanksgiving festival * April 21–22: {{ill|lt=Reisai|Q11385469|qid=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} (21st Dōza-sai, 22nd formal festival and divine procession) - three karakuri-float {{ill|lt=dashi|Q11470974|qid=Q11470974|1=Matsuri float}}巡行. * May 5: {{ill|lt=Kokufu Festival|Q11420919|qid=Q11420919|1=Kokufu-sai}} (Kōno-machi) - this shrine is known for “'''chimaki throwing'''” and “'''wild mikoshi'''” at Kamisoroi-yama (Kamitsu-yama). * 3rd Saturday and Sunday of May: Magatama Festival * June 30: summer {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|qid=Q11261910|1=Ōharae-shiki}} * Sunday nearest October 17: main festival, memorial festival * Late November: Sake Festival * November 23: New grain labor thanksgiving festival * December 20: year-end great purification === Access === * {{ill|lt=Odakyu Electric Railway|Q83584|qid=Q83584|lt=Odakyu|1=Odakyu Electric Railway}}{{ill|lt=Odakyu Odawara Line|Q135176|qid=Q135176|lt=Odawara Line|1=Odakyū Odawara Line}}{{ill|lt=Isehara Station|Q4205219|qid=Q4205219|1=Isehara Station}} north exit, then {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Chūō Kōtsū|Q10854960|qid=Q10854960|1=Kanagawa Chuo Kotsu}} bus ** {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Chūō Kōtsū Isehara Depot#Isehara Station North Exit - Isehara Depot - Kurihara direction|Q11588882|qid=Q11588882|lt=Route I16|1=Route I16}} to “Kurihara,” get off at “Hibita Jinja” stop, 2 minutes on foot. ** {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Chūō Kōtsū Isehara Depot#Isehara Station North Exit - Tonomura - Bishamon-ike - Isehara Depot direction|Q11588882|qid=Q11588882|lt=Route I41|1=Route I41}} to “Isehara Depot,” get off at “Sannomiya” stop, 7 minutes on foot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * {{ill|lt=Ashida Itojin|Q17988362|qid=Q17988362|1=Koreto Ashida}} (ed.), '{{ill|lt=Dai Nihon Chishi Taikei|Q11435652|qid=Q11435652|1=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei}} 17 {{ill|lt=Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudokikō|Q11503942|qid=Q11503942|1=Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudoki-kō}} Vol. 3' ({{ill|lt=Yūzankaku|Q11659206|qid=Q11659206|lt=Yūzankaku|1=Yuzankaku}}, Sept. 1962) ** {{cite book|ja|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第38巻新編相模国風土記稿3巻之50村里部大住郡巻之9|chapter=糟屋庄 三ノ宮村 三宮明神社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/55}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} * {{ill|lt=Kuroita Katsumi|Q3894478|qid=Q3894478|1=Katsumi Kuroita}}, Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee (ed.), '{{ill|lt=Kokushi Taikei|Q10926780|qid=Q10926780|lt=Kokushi Taikei|1=Kokushi Taikei}} Vol. 32 {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Azuma Kagami|1=UNKNOWN}} first part' ({{ill|lt=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755|qid=Q4346755|lt=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}}, July 1964) * Kuroita Katsumi, Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee (ed.), 'Kokushi Taikei Vol. 33 Azuma Kagami second part' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, Feb. 1965) * National Shrine Directory Publication Society Historical Center (ed.), 'National Shrine Directory Vol. 1' (July 1977) * Shintō Taikei Editorial Committee (ed.), 'Shintō Taikei Shrine Section 16: Suruga, Izu, Kai, Sagami' (Mar. 1980) * {{ill|lt=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|qid=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} (ed.), 'Nihon no Kamigami - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 11 Kantō' ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|qid=Q4346470|lt=Hakusuisha|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}, Dec. 1984) * Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System Study Group (ed.), 'Basic Study of the Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System' (Iwata Shoin, Feb. 2000) === Related items === {{commonscat|Hibita-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Sagami Province|Q11582137|qid=Q11582137|1=List of Shikinaisha in Sagami Province}} * {{ill|lt=Racchimen Kofun|Q11426332|qid=Q11426332|1=Racchimen Kofun}} - about 200 meters northwest of this shrine. According to documents, the shrine used to stand where this kofun is. * {{ill|lt=Hibita Shrine|Q11547458|qid=Q11547458|1=Hibita Shrine (Q11547458)}} (Koyasu Myōjin) - one of the competing claim shrines for Engishiki “Hibita Shrine.” * {{ill|lt=Hibita Village|Q11547362|qid=Q11547362|1=Hibita}} - former village named after this shrine. === External links === * [http://hibita.jp/ Hibita Shrine official website] * {{Official blog}} * {{Facebook}} * {{Twitter}} * {{Instagram}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=736&cd=1209179&scd=&npg=0 Hibita Shrine] (Kanagawa Shrine Office) * [https://travelog-jpn.blogspot.com/2011/06/8.html Walking the roads of Sagami: Sagami Sannomiya Hibita Shrine] {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hibita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hibita Shrine]][[Category:San-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sagami Province]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Municipal History of Isehara]] [[Category:伊勢原市の建築物]] [[Category:Sagami Province]]
